label: Orders Table
description: Full orders table with integrated filters, sorting, status badges, and inline cancel and amend actions.

0.1.note: WidgetErrorBoundary wraps all widgets in widget-wrapper.tsx line 89

0.4.note: no any or @ts-ignore; as unknown as Record<string,unknown>[] cast on export data is a type-safe narrowing

0.5.note: widget imports only from context, shared, and ui components; no lib/mocks imports

0.6.note: isLoading passed to TableWidget which renders skeleton

0.7.note: emptyMessage="No orders match your filters" passed to TableWidget

0.8.note: error={error ? "Failed to load orders" : null} + onRetry={refetch} passed to TableWidget

0.9.note: no commented-out blocks or unreachable branches

l0.notes: All 9 checks pass. No fixes required.

1.1.note: all data (filteredOrders, filters, actions) exclusively from useOrdersData()

1.3.note: scopedOrders filtered by scopeStrategyIds derived from globalScope.organizationIds

1.4.note: getStrategyIdsForScope accepts clientIds; cascades through org→client→strategy

1.5.note: local strategyFilter + globalScope.strategyIds both applied in context

1.6.note: scopeStrategyIds.length === 0 skips filter, returns all orders

1.7.note: widget has no inline reference data; ASSET_CLASS_OPTIONS from context is a domain-type constant

l1.notes: Context (read-only): lib/mocks imports are dev-mode data sources in the context layer, not in the widget. Acceptable per spec.

l2.notes: Globally satisfied by commits 32ba8ab ({domain,tier} entitlements across all 17 register.ts files + 5 PageEntitlementGate wrappers) and f9f29ea (65 automated tests). Re-audit only if wiring test fails or entitlements change.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ (all order statuses), paper ✓ (shows SIMULATED tagged orders), batch ✓ (only FILLED orders shown). Context applies both isPaper and isBatch to scopedOrders.

3.2.note: orders-data-context.tsx line 146: const { isPaper, isBatch } = useExecutionMode(). scopedOrders memo (lines 253-271) applies paper tagging and batch filtering. Reads mode from execution-mode-context, not scope.mode directly — equivalent.

3.3.note: Human: in batch mode, verify cancel and amend action buttons are absent or disabled. Batch mode filters to FILLED orders only (all non-actionable statuses), so isActionable() returns false — cancel/amend should not render. Human to confirm.

3.4.note: Human: verify batch/historical mode indicator is visible in layout header.

3.5.note: Paper mode behavior defined: order IDs tagged with '(Paper)' suffix; status set to SIMULATED. Widget renders these via TableWidget with SIMULATED status badge. Expected UX: SIMULATED badge in status column, paper-tagged order IDs.

3.6.note: Widget has no WebSocket or interval polling. Refetch is on-demand via user action or ledger events.

l3.notes: Agent checks 3.1/3.2/3.5/3.6 complete. Human to verify 3.3/3.4 in browser.

4.1.note: Cancel onClick calls cancelOrder(orderId) — real mutation handler from context (cancelMutation.mutate). Amend onClick calls openAmendDialog(order) — real state setter. No dead handlers.

4.2.note: Instrument links use getInstrumentRoute() which returns valid routes (/services/trading/terminal, /services/trading/options, /services/trading/defi, /services/trading/sports). 'View Strategy' link uses /services/trading/strategies/${strategyFilter} — dynamic [id] segment exists at app/(platform)/services/trading/strategies/[id]. No href='#' found.

4.3.note: Human: verify amend dialog shows qty and price fields and all necessary controls for an amend workflow.

4.4.note: Human: verify cancel produces toast/order removal; amend produces confirmation and row update.

4.5.note: All route targets verified to exist: /services/trading/terminal ✓, /services/trading/options ✓, /services/trading/defi ✓, /services/trading/sports ✓, /services/trading/strategies/[id] ✓.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks inside render cycle. requiredEntitlements declared only in register.ts; gate handled by WidgetWrapper. No per-render entitlement evaluation.

4.7.note: No tabs or accordions in widget body; amend dialog is a modal, not a collapsible.

4.8.note: Human: verify table scrolls within widget bounds at minH=5 without overflow.

l4.notes: Agent checks 4.1/4.2/4.5/4.6 complete. Human to verify 4.3/4.4/4.8.

5.1.note: Target strategies: all — widget is cross-domain. Displays orders for any strategy type (DeFi basis-trade, recursive-staked-basis, AMM-LP, lending-arb, liquidation-monitor; CeFi market-making; TradFi futures; Sports predictions). classifyInstrument() handles Spot/Perp/Futures/Options/DeFi/Prediction asset classes. Filter by strategy_id directly in context.

l5.notes: 5.2-5.6 require human walkthrough covering: cancel flow, amend flow, filter cascade, cross-asset-class order rendering.

l6.notes: Deferred — L6 test authoring not in scope for this pass.

7.1.note: Zero hex/rgb/hsl literals in widget file. CSS var() refs in bracket notation (text-[var(--pnl-positive)]) are correct design-token usage, not violations.

7.2.note: No lib/mocks imports in widget file.

7.3.note: 462 lines — within 500-line limit. buildColumns() is 263 lines (exceeds 200-line method limit).

7.4.note: OrdersTableWidget PascalCase; helper functions camelCase; file kebab-case. No naming violations.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.6.note: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML, eval, new Function, or innerHTML= in widget file.

7.7.note: columns memo (line 377) deps on [cancelOrder, openAmendDialog] — both are stable useCallback refs from context. No inline object/array literals in effect/memo deps.

l7.notes: buildColumns() at 263 lines marginally exceeds the 200-line function limit. Extracting sub-renderers would improve maintainability but is not a blocking violation for this pass.

8.1.note: All text-[10px] occurrences replaced with text-xs (lines 199, 213, 227, 252). Remaining text-[var(--...)] are CSS token references — correct design-system usage.

8.2.note: Zero arbitrary spacing (p-[...], m-[...], gap-[...]) in widget file.

8.3.note: Uses Badge from components/ui/badge, Button from components/ui/button, Link from next/link, TableWidget shared primitive. No bare div+tailwind substitutes for available primitives.

8.4.note:
Agent: no without alt, no bare , no
. Amend dialog uses Dialog+Input+Label from UI primitives (accessible). Human: keyboard-only nav of table + cancel/amend flow + screen-reader pass needed.

8.5.note: Human: verify renders correctly at minW=6/minH=5; no horizontal scroll at 1280/1920.

l8.notes: Agent-executable 8.1-8.3 fully pass (4 text-[10px] fixed in this session). Human to complete 8.4 a11y walkthrough and 8.5 resize test.

keepSeparate.rationale: KPI strip and orders table are separate widget instances in the same preset. Strip (1 row) and table (10 rows) have incompatible minH values making merging impractical. Keeping separate also allows independent placement in custom layouts.

relationships.notes: Peer of orders-kpi-strip: both consume OrdersDataProvider; neither drives the other. Instrument links navigate to terminal/options/defi/sports tabs — those pages are independent, not detail widgets driven by this one.

dataContract.notes: Data via shared OrdersDataProvider calling useOrders() (REST). Ledger changes trigger window events (mock-order-filled, mock-ledger-reset) → refetch. Production path: REST GET /api/orders; cancel/amend via POST mutations. WebSocket upgrade path anticipated for live order state updates.

gaps: buildColumns() exceeds 200-line function limit — refactor to sub-renderers recommended
Amend action button color (text-blue-400) has no design-token equivalent — coordinator to add --action-amend (or --status-info-subtle). Cancel migrated to --destructive token 2026-04-21.
Human walkthrough (cancel, amend, filter cascade, cross-asset) not yet completed
coverage.notes: classifyInstrument() covers Spot, Perp, Futures, Options, DeFi (AAVE/Uniswap/Lido/Morpho), Prediction (Betfair/Polymarket/Kalshi/NBA/NFL/EPL/LaLiga). Full cross-domain coverage by design.

knownIssues: buildColumns() function is 263 lines (limit 200) — marginally over; low priority split

findings.description: orders-table-widget.tsx:57 — buildColumns() function is approximately 263 lines, exceeding the 200-line method limit
findings.deferReason: Low risk — column defs are declarative config, not complex logic. Split would add indirection without benefit. Deferred to future refactor.

findings.description: orders-table-widget.tsx — ~163 user-visible strings not wrapped in i18n calls
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21

findings.description: orders-table-widget.tsx:199,213,227 — text-[10px] on client_name, category, strategy_family empty-state spans; line 252 — text-[10px] on fill percent
findings.fix: Replaced all 4 occurrences of text-[10px] with text-xs (Tailwind scale, 12px)

findings.description: orders-table-widget.tsx:22 — border-[var(--status-error)] text-[var(--status-error)] references a CSS var not defined in globals.css
findings.fix: Replaced border-[var(--status-error)] text-[var(--status-error)] with border-status-critical text-status-critical (existing --status-critical token via @theme inline --color-status-critical)

findings.description: orders-table-widget.tsx:155,171 — text-emerald-400/text-rose-400 used for P&L and edge coloring instead of design tokens var(--pnl-positive)/var(--pnl-negative)
findings.fix: Replaced text-emerald-400/text-rose-400 with semantic utilities text-pnl-positive/text-pnl-negative (resolved via @theme inline --color-pnl-positive/--color-pnl-negative; Tailwind v4 resolves semantic utilities from @theme tokens)

findings.description: orders-table-widget.tsx:291 — Cancel button used text-rose-400/hover:bg-rose-400/10 (no token). Amend button uses text-blue-400/hover:bg-blue-400/10 (no token).
findings.fix: Cancel button migrated to existing --destructive token: text-destructive/hover:bg-destructive/10 (2026-04-21).
findings.deferReason: Amend button still uses text-blue-400/hover:bg-blue-400/10 — no --action-info/--action-amend token exists in globals.css. Coordinator to add a non-destructive informational action token (e.g., --action-amend or --status-info-subtle) so amend affordance can be migrated.
